title: Crunchy Malaysian Walnut Flatbread
description: A light, flaky flatbread studded with toasted walnuts, brightened with malt vinegar for a subtle tang, perfect as a vegan snack or side.

ingredients.name: sunflower oil
ingredients.notes: for dough plus extra for coating

instructions: Toast the walnuts in a dry skillet over medium heat for 3-4 minutes, then finely chop.
Whisk the flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda, sugar and cumin in a large bowl.
Add the malt vinegar and sunflower oil to the dry mix, stirring to combine.
Gradually pour in the water, mixing until a sticky dough forms; it should be firm enough to hold its shape.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently for 2-3 minutes.
Shape the dough into a flat disc (about 12 inches in diameter) and transfer to a parchment‑lined tray.
Cover the dough loosely with plastic wrap and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes to allow the leavening to develop.
Preheat a non‑stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly oil the surface.
Place the rested dough in the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, pressing gently with a spatula until the surface is golden and flaky.
Flip, coat lightly with oil, and cook until both sides are crisp and the interior is cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove from heat, slice into wedges, and serve warm or at room temperature. Enjoy the nutty crunch!
